dimension: D16 Completeness — whole-category blind spots not covered by D1-D15
summary: The planning set is unusually thorough on the dimensions already audited (flows, state machines, schemas, source-of-truth, security, deployment). But several WHOLE CATEGORIES of risk are absent rather than merely under-refined. The most load-bearing gap is determinism/RNG capture for replay: replay is a first-class, must-ship invariant (REQ-F-014, DATA_MODEL Replay Rules), yet mutation, parent-selection tie-breaks, and fusion all involve randomness that is never specified as captured-into-events — so a "replay" can silently diverge from the original run, which directly threatens the acceptance proof. Other genuine blind spots: clock/timestamp authority for replay ordering; runtime self-observability (kernel logs/metrics, distinct from Langfuse LLM traces); configuration/env validation and fail-fast; data-retention/PII handling for persisted idea content shipped to Langfuse Cloud and Neo4j; the seed/Gen-0 agenome authoring process (the genesis of the proof is hand-waved); event/schema/scoring-policy migration during a fast-iterating 2-week build; the Energy-to-real-cost accounting model itself; multi-run concurrency on one Postgres; and dashboard accessibility for the projector-based showcase. None of these require production-SaaS scope; most are small but currently unowned.

findings.finding: Determinism/RNG capture for replay is an entire missing category. Replay is a must-ship invariant and the demo safety net, but mutation (which fields change, by how much), parent-selection tie-breaking, fusion crossover choices, and any sampling are randomness-driven. Nothing requires the random decisions of the ORIGINAL run to be persisted as events. 'Replay uses persisted events and stored outputs, not fresh model calls' covers LLM output but not kernel-internal randomness, so a replay can diverge from the run it claims to reproduce — quietly invalidating the 'Gen N+1 beats Gen N' proof.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md 'Replay Rules' + 'Required Event Types' (agenome.mutated/agenome.fused only persist a 'summary'); DOMAIN_MODEL.md Invariants ('Replay uses persisted events and stored outputs, not fresh model calls'); REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-F-014/REQ-T-003
findings.proposed_fix: Add an architecture invariant: all kernel non-determinism is either (a) seeded from a per-run seed persisted in run.configured and reproduced deterministically, or (b) its concrete outcome (exact mutated fields/values, chosen parents, crossover points) is persisted in the agenome.mutated/agenome.fused/lineage.culled payloads. Replay must reconstruct from those persisted outcomes, never re-sample. Add a test that a replay reproduces identical agenome traits and selection decisions.

findings.finding: Clock/timestamp authority for replay is unspecified — a missing category, not a refinement of the state machine. occurredAt is a free-form string and replay must 'preserve original timestamps and event ordering', but no doc says who stamps time (DB now() vs app clock vs worker host), what timezone/format is canonical, or that sequence (not occurredAt) is the sole ordering key. With in-process workers and possible concurrent runs, app-clock skew or non-UTC stamps can produce out-of-order or ambiguous timelines in the fitness-over-time chart.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md Event Envelope (occurredAt: string) and 'sequence is monotonic per run'; USER_FLOWS.md 'Replay A Run' ('Preserve original timestamps and event ordering'); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §8
findings.proposed_fix: Lock: occurredAt is UTC ISO-8601 stamped by a single authority (recommend Postgres at append time); ordering is by (runId, sequence) only and occurredAt is display/analytics-only and never used for ordering. State this in ARCHITECTURE.md §7 and the event-store contract.

findings.finding: Runtime self-observability (kernel logs/metrics/health) is an uncovered category. All observability discussion is about LLM traces via Langfuse. There is no plan for structured kernel logging, worker liveness/health, run-progress metrics, or how an operator diagnoses a stuck/hung run during the 10-minute showcase. When a live run stalls, the demo decision (continue vs switch to replay) needs a runtime signal Langfuse cannot give.
findings.evidence: DECISIONS.md ADR-005 and RESEARCH.md R-004 (Langfuse scoped to LLM traces/cost/eval); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §10 lists job responsibilities but no logging/metrics; no kernel-log mention anywhere in docs/planning
findings.proposed_fix: Add a thin 'runtime observability' subsection: structured kernel logs (run/generation/agenome correlation IDs), a GET /runs/:id health/progress field (current generation, candidates in flight, last event time, caps consumed), and a worker-alive heartbeat event. Keep it local-only/console+Postgres; no external metrics stack needed for MVP.

findings.finding: Seed / Generation-0 agenome authoring is an unowned category that the proof depends on. The whole acceptance claim is 'later generation beats earlier generation', which requires a well-defined Gen 0. How seed agenomes are authored, how many, by whom, where they live (config? code? Rule of Cool port?), and how their traits are validated against the Agenome schema is left entirely to an open question. A weak or accidentally-strong Gen 0 can make the improvement claim meaningless.
findings.evidence: ASSUMPTIONS.md A-009 and 'Rule of Cool ... implementation details are not yet available'; OPEN_QUESTIONS.md OQ-010; DECISIONS.md open question 'Rule of Cool reuse depth'; PRODUCT_BRIEF.md core workflow step 2; no seed-authoring spec in ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md
findings.proposed_fix: Add a 'Seed Agenome Authoring' subsection: define a checked-in seed-agenome set (config files under config/seed-agenomes) authored by hand against the Agenome contract, validated by the same schema validation as child agenomes, with a documented diversity rationale. Treat Rule of Cool as conceptual reference only (per A-009 fallback). Owner: selection/ML surface.

findings.finding: Data retention / PII / content-sensitivity for persisted idea content is uncovered. Append-only event log stores live audience prompts and 'current signals' for zeitgeist synthesis forever, and the same content is shipped to Langfuse Cloud (external) and exported to Neo4j during the spike. The threat model only covers SECRETS redaction, never user/idea content sensitivity, retention, or what happens to a live audience-supplied prompt after the showcase.
findings.evidence: THREAT_MODEL.md (controls are secret-redaction only); DATA_MODEL.md candidate fields (title/summary/claims) and ZeitgeistSynthesisPayload.currentSignals; OPEN_QUESTIONS.md OQ-012 (audience prompt); ADR-005 ships prompt/output metadata to Langfuse Cloud
findings.proposed_fix: Add a one-line MVP retention/content policy: idea content is non-sensitive demo data, audience-entered prompts are treated as ephemeral demo input, and Langfuse content logging can be toggled off if an audience prompt is sensitive. State explicitly that no PII is expected and operators must not enter sensitive prompts. Flag anything beyond this as deferred.

findings.finding: Event-schema and scoring-policy migration during the build is an uncovered category. schemaVersion and policyVersion fields exist, but in a fast-iterating 2-week build the event payload and scoring rubric WILL change repeatedly, and replay/rehearsal fixtures are persisted across those changes. There is no plan for what happens when a previously-recorded run (the demo fallback) was written under an older schemaVersion/policyVersion — risking a broken replay on showcase day.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md Event Envelope (schemaVersion) and FitnessScore (policy version); DATA_MODEL.md open question 'Exact event payload fields after provider/model spike'; ADR-003 fallback 'normalize projections later'; no migration/back-compat policy in any doc
findings.proposed_fix: Add a rule: replay readers must tolerate or version-gate older schemaVersion/policyVersion payloads, and the demo-fallback run must be re-recorded after any breaking event-schema change (add to the rehearsal checklist in §14). Pin the demo-fallback run's schemaVersion in the rehearsal script.

findings.finding: The Energy-to-real-cost accounting MODEL is undefined (distinct from cap enforcement, which D3/D15 cover). EnergyEvent records amount/unit/reason, and energy is 'compute/token/tool/spawn budget', but nothing specifies how energy is computed (estimated vs actual provider tokens), when the ledger is debited (before vs after a call returns), or how estimate/actual reconciliation works — which affects both cap correctness and the 'energy scarcity as selection pressure' demo evidence (REQ-E-004).
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md EnergyEvent row and energy.spent event; DOMAIN_MODEL.md Energy noun; REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-E-004; REQUIREMENTS.md implied req 'treat LLM/tool spend as first-class metered events, even if real provider cost tracking is approximate'
findings.proposed_fix: Add a short energy-accounting note: define the energy unit (e.g., normalized token-equivalents), debit pre-call with an estimate then reconcile post-call against returned usage, and persist both estimate and actual on energy.spent. Acceptable to be approximate for MVP but state the model so caps and the energy chart are consistent.

findings.finding: Multi-run concurrency on one Postgres / in-process worker is an uncovered category. The single-operator assumption is interpreted as 'one run at a time', but rehearsals plus a hosted instance plus the live demo can produce concurrent runs against one DB and one in-process worker pool. sequence is per-run (safe), but projection-rebuild contention, connection-pool limits, and whether the kernel serializes runs or runs them concurrently are unspecified.
findings.evidence: ASSUMPTIONS.md A-001 (single operator) and Phase 8 'single operator/admin role'; ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §10 ('in-process workers'); no concurrency-model statement anywhere
findings.proposed_fix: State the MVP concurrency posture explicitly: e.g., 'kernel supports N concurrent runs bounded by a global run-concurrency cap; projections are per-run and rebuilt independently; one shared Postgres connection pool sized to caps.' Even 'one active run at a time, enforced by the kernel' is acceptable if stated.

findings.finding: Configuration/env validation and fail-fast is uncovered. Config files (model registry, scoring policy, runtime caps, demo problem sets) and env-only credentials are listed, but there is no statement that they are schema-validated at boot, that precedence (env vs file vs defaults) is defined, or that the app fails fast on missing/invalid provider keys — a common cause of a dead live demo.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §13 'Config files' list and 'provider credentials via environment only'; no config-validation or boot-check requirement in any doc
findings.proposed_fix: Add: all config files are Zod/JSON-Schema validated at startup; required env (provider keys, DB URL) is checked at boot with a clear fail-fast error; document config precedence (defaults < file < env). Add a 'config loads and validates' smoke to the rehearsal checklist.

findings.finding: Dashboard accessibility and presentation robustness for the showcase is uncovered. The dashboard is a first-class acceptance surface shown on a projector to an audience, yet there is no mention of colorblind-safe encoding for lineage/fitness/energy states (survive/die, novelty-high/feasibility-low), contrast/legibility at projector distance, or non-color status cues — all of which affect whether reviewers can actually read the proof.
findings.evidence: REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-UX-001/REQ-E-002 (lineage specialization shown via labels/highlights/color); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §11 frontend list; EVALUATION_CRITERIA.md demo evidence; no accessibility/contrast guidance anywhere
findings.proposed_fix: Add a brief UX note: status must use shape/label/icon in addition to color (colorblind-safe palette), high-contrast theme suitable for projector, and readable font sizes. Cheap to honor if stated before the dashboard is built.

findings.finding: Is concurrent run execution in scope for the demo, or is the kernel allowed to serialize to exactly one active run at a time? This decision shapes the concurrency model, connection pooling, and worker design, and is currently implicit.
findings.evidence: ASSUMPTIONS.md A-001 (single operator) vs ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §10 in-process workers; no explicit run-concurrency decision
findings.proposed_fix: Decide and record. Recommended default: MVP serializes to one active run at a time (kernel-enforced), with replay viewable concurrently with a live run since replay is read-only. This minimizes concurrency risk while preserving rehearsal-during-demo.

findings.finding: If a live audience prompt is used at the showcase, is its content allowed to be sent to Langfuse Cloud (external) and persisted in the append-only log indefinitely? This is a content-policy decision the docs leave open.
findings.evidence: OPEN_QUESTIONS.md OQ-012 (audience prompt); ADR-005 (content metadata to Langfuse Cloud); DATA_MODEL.md append-only log persists prompt-derived content
findings.proposed_fix: Decide and record. Recommended default: treat audience prompts as non-sensitive demo input, allow Langfuse logging, but provide an operator toggle to disable external content logging and to use a prepared prompt if an audience-supplied prompt is sensitive.
